response.description_long:
Prior authorization is the top administrative burden in specialty care. Staff spend hours on payer portals and phone calls; patients wait days for approvals; denials arrive for missing documentation that was sitting in the chart all along. Clinicore automates prior authorization for specialty clinics by reading orders from the EHR, assembling payer-specific documentation, submitting electronically, and tracking every request to a decision.

Clinicore integrates with Epic, Cerner, and athenahealth. When a clinician places an order that needs authorization, Clinicore detects it, pulls the clinical notes and labs that the payer's policy requires, and assembles the submission. A rules engine encodes payer policies and keeps them current; a clinical operations team reviews edge cases before anything goes out. Status updates flow back into the EHR, and staff see one queue instead of a dozen portals.

The platform screens every order against payer and plan: orders that do not require authorization are marked cleared, and orders that do enter an assembly pipeline with urgency flags. For each authorization, the rules engine maps the payer's policy to a checklist of required evidence and pulls each item from the chart. Missing items are flagged before submission. Packets submit electronically wherever the payer supports it; the operations team handles portal and fax submission where it does not. Every request is tracked to a decision, with status flowing back into the EHR. When a denial occurs, Clinicore assembles the appeal from the same evidence pipeline, attaches the payer's policy citation, and tracks the appeal to resolution. Denial analytics show patterns by payer, code, and policy.

Clinicore serves specialty practices and clinic groups in oncology, cardiology, rheumatology, and imaging — from single-site clinics to groups with 40 locations. Use cases include infusion and radiation authorizations with staging and prior-therapy evidence for oncology; imaging and device authorizations with appropriate-use criteria for cardiology; specialty-pharmacy coordination for biologics and step-therapy documentation for rheumatology; and high-volume screening against payer rules for imaging.

The company was founded in 2020 in Nashville, TN by a practice administrator and two health-data engineers. The founding insight was that the evidence payers wanted was already in the chart, yet staff were paid to move it through a fax machine. Clinicore is 60 people, remote-friendly, with clinical operations concentrated in Nashville.

Clinics using Clinicore report authorizations submitted in minutes instead of hours, first-pass approval rates above 90 percent, and staff time on authorizations cut by more than half. A 12-site oncology group saw their authorization backlog go from three days to same-day within two months of go-live, and their authorization team moved from portal work to exception handling. An imaging network cut authorization-related reschedules by two thirds. Clinicore is priced per provider per month with volume pricing for groups — no per-transaction fees, no charge for appeals.
